nodes.label: Reader-first public home
nodes.rationale: The root route serves public readers first while member access remains a quiet link to the portal.

nodes.label: Single portal shell
nodes.rationale: A single shell with module tabs preserves context and is easier to navigate than many disconnected member pages.

nodes.label: Plain and specific interface writing
nodes.rationale: Short labels, active verbs, and concrete nouns keep the interface clear and avoid promotional or generic AI-style wording.

nodes.label: Unna and Source Sans 3 web fonts
Member event and survey script bundle
Light and dark semantic theme tokens
Application architecture key rules
nodes.rationale: Stable route boundaries, adapter seams, isolated Cloudflare bindings, and shared developer access keep the app portable across local, shared, and production modes.

nodes.label: D1, SQLite, and shared API database adapters
R2, local file, and shared API storage adapters
Focused adapter extension pattern
nodes.rationale: New backends implement the existing focused interface and are selected centrally so feature code remains backend-independent.

nodes.label: Auth.js v5 Google OAuth and database sessions
Lightweight middleware decision
nodes.rationale: Middleware only redirects obvious unauthenticated portal requests because deployed D1 access was not proven and Node middleware is not supported by the Cloudflare build.

nodes.label: Authoritative server-side auth and role checks
Clean reset path for empty D1 databases
nodes.rationale: Remote dev and production contained no user rows, so a clean reset avoids preserving an obsolete schema, subject to explicit command approval.

nodes.label: Members table as Auth.js user table
Server, database, and storage infrastructure layers
Cloudflare context import boundary
nodes.rationale: Only infrastructure and adapter code may access Cloudflare context so route handlers and features remain portable.

nodes.label: Shared developer Cloudflare isolation
nodes.rationale: Outside developers receive app-level shared access instead of Cloudflare account access, production credentials, or raw SQL capabilities.

nodes.label: Secret file commit and ignore policy
Production storage binding lock
nodes.rationale: Production defaults to the native R2 binding and permits an override only for a documented emergency.

nodes.label: Wrangler command approval gate
nodes.rationale: Exact Wrangler commands must be shown and approved before schema resets, migrations, seeds, or production database operations.

nodes.label: Schema and contract first migration order
nodes.rationale: Schema changes land first, followed by permissions, auth, repositories, contracts, and seed data because each later layer consumes names introduced by the earlier layer.

nodes.label: V5 schema additions and deferred subsystem removal
Retention and member admin permission model
Retention repository and deferred repository removal
Super admin roster bypass
nodes.rationale: Existing super admins bypass roster membership so a stale or misconfigured roster cannot lock out the organization administrators.

nodes.label: Local and remote migration approval gates
nodes.rationale: Migration generation may run locally, but applying migrations or redeploying the shared Worker pauses for explicit approval with exact commands.
